title: Spring by William Morris
content:
Spring am I, too soft of heart
Much to speak ere I depart:
Ask the Summer-tide to prove
The abundance of my love.
meaning: "Spring" by William Morris is a love and nature english poem consisting of 4 lines. This English poem by William Morris demonstrates the timeless power of verse to capture complex human emotions. Beginning with "Spring am I, too soft of heart Much to speak ere I depart:...", this piece explores themes of love and nature through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. William Morris's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
